structured.name: Cold Boot Charging Station
structured.objective:
The objective of this use case is to enable a Charging Station that is powering up to register itself
at a CSMS and provide the right state information.
structured.description:
This use case describes how the CSMS can control which Charging Stations access it. To be able
to control Charging Stations connecting to a CSMS, Charging Stations are required to send
BootNotificationRequest. This request contains some general information about the Charging
Station.
scenario: 1. The Charging Station is powered up.
2. The Charging Station sends BootNotificationRequest to the CSMS.
3. The CSMS returns with BootNotificationResponse with the status Accepted.

4. Optional: The Charging Station sends StatusNotificationRequest with status Unavailable to the
CSMS for each Connector.

5. The Charging Station sends StatusNotificationRequest to the CSMS for each Connector. If the
status was set to Unavailable or Reserved from the CSMS prior to the (re)boot, the Connector
should return to this status, otherwise the status should be Available or, when it resumes a
transaction that was ongoing, the status should be Occupied.
7. The Charging Station sends HeartbeatRequest to the CSMS.
structured.prerequisites: The Charging Station is powered down.
structured.postconditions:
Successful postcondition:
The Charging Station is in Idle status, and Accepted.
Failure postcondition:
The Charging Station received the status Rejected, B03 - Cold Boot Charging Station -Rejected
applies.
The Charging Station received the status Pending, B02 - Cold Boot Charging Station - Pending
applies.
structured.error_handling:
1. No initial establishment of connection of communication between the CSMS and Charging
Station: Retry Connection with the CSMS.
2. No response / time-out from the CSMS: The Charging Station resends BootNotificationRequest
after a waiting interval. The Charging Station chooses this interval on its own (since it dit not get a
BootNotificationResponse containing this interval), in a way that avoids flooding the CSMS with
requests.
structured.remarks:
Multiple options for a self check are possible: some Charging Stations boot and send status
notifications with Unavailable, then perform a check of all the hardware and send new
NotifyEvents with AvailabilityState Available when the Charging Station is up and running.
However, there is no required order for a self check and sending a BootNotificationRequest. A
Charging Stations can also do the self check before sending a BootNotificationRequest and
determine the status before a (mobile) network connection is established and a
BootNotificationRequest is sent.
When something is wrong with the Charging Station or EVSE, the status SHALL be set to Faulted.
Reserved and Unavailable states persist after a reboot.

requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL send
BootNotificationRequest to the CSMS with
information about its configuration.

requirements.precondition:
B01.FR.01
The CSMS has received
BootNotificationRequest from the
Charging Station.
requirements.definition:
The CSMS SHALL respond to indicate whether it
will accept the Charging Station.

requirements.precondition:
After a reboot (for instance due to a
remote reset command, power
outage, firmware update, software
error etc.)
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL again connect to the
CSMS and SHALL send a BootNotificationRequest
each time it boots or reboots.

requirements.precondition:
When the CSMS responds with
BootNotificationResponse with the
status Accepted AND
interval > 0
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL adjust the heartbeat
interval in accordance with the interval from the
response message.

requirements.precondition:
When the CSMS responds with
BootNotificationResponse with the
status Accepted.
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL send a
StatusNotificationRequest for each Connector with
its current state.

requirements.precondition:
The Charging Station has received
BootNotificationResponse.
AND
Charging Station is configured to use
Heartbeats for time synchronization
TimeSource
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL synchronize the
Charging Station’s internal clock with the supplied
CSMS’s current time.

requirements.precondition:
When a Charging Station or an EVSE is
set to status Unavailable by a
Change Availability command.
requirements.definition:
The Unavailable status MUST be persistent
across reboots.

requirements.precondition:
Between the physical power-on/reboot
and the successful completion of a
BootNotification, where the CSMS
returns Accepted or Pending.
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL NOT send any other
OCPP requests to the CSMS (except
BootNotificationRequest). This includes cached
OCPP messages that are still present in the
Charging Station from prior sessions.

The Charging Station SHALL indicate the reason for
sending the BootNotificationRequest message in
the reason field.

requirements.precondition:
The Charging Station has received a
BootNotificationResponse in which
status is not Accepted AND
the Charging Station sends a RPC
Framework: CALL message that is
NOT a BootNotificationRequest or a
message triggered by one of the
following messages:
TriggerMessageRequest,
GetBaseReportRequest,
GetReportRequest.
requirements.definition:
The CSMS SHALL respond with RPC Framework:
CALLERROR: SecurityError.

requirements.precondition:
B01.FR.01 AND
Security profile 3 is used
requirements.definition:
The CSMS SHALL check the SerialNumber in the
BootNotificationRequest against the Serial Number
in the Certificate Common Name.

requirements.precondition:
B01.FR.11 AND
the SerialNumber in the
BootNotificationRequest does NOT
equal the Serial Number in the
Certificate Common Name
requirements.definition: The CSMS SHALL close WebSocket connection.

requirements.precondition: When an EVSE has been reserved
requirements.definition:
The Reserved state MUST be persistent across
reboots.

chunks.content:
# B01 - Cold Boot Charging Station

**Objective:** The objective of this use case is to enable a Charging Station that is powering up to register itself
at a CSMS and provide the right state information.

**Description:** This use case describes how the CSMS can control which Charging Stations access it. To be able
to control Charging Stations connecting to a CSMS, Charging Stations are required to send
BootNotificationRequest. This request contains some general information about the Charging
Station.

**Actors:** Charging Station, CSMS

**Scenario:**
1. The Charging Station is powered up.
2. The Charging Station sends BootNotificationRequest to the CSMS.
3. The CSMS returns with BootNotificationResponse with the status Accepted.
4. Optional: The Charging Station sends StatusNotificationRequest with status Unavailable to the
CSMS for each Connector.
5. The Charging Station sends StatusNotificationRequest to the CSMS for each Connector. If the
status was set to Unavailable or Reserved from the CSMS prior to the (re)boot, the Connector
should return to this status, otherwise the status should be Available or, when it resumes a
transaction that was ongoing, the status should be Occupied.
6. Normal operation is resumed.
7. The Charging Station sends HeartbeatRequest to the CSMS.

**Prerequisites:** The Charging Station is powered down.

**Postconditions:** Successful postcondition:
The Charging Station is in Idle status, and Accepted.
Failure postcondition:
The Charging Station received the status Rejected, B03 - Cold Boot Charging Station -Rejected
applies.
The Charging Station received the status Pending, B02 - Cold Boot Charging Station - Pending
applies.

**Error handling:** 1. No initial establishment of connection of communication between the CSMS and Charging
Station: Retry Connection with the CSMS.
2. No response / time-out from the CSMS: The Charging Station resends BootNotificationRequest
after a waiting interval. The Charging Station chooses this interval on its own (since it dit not get a
BootNotificationResponse containing this interval), in a way that avoids flooding the CSMS with
requests.

**Remarks:** Multiple options for a self check are possible: some Charging Stations boot and send status
notifications with Unavailable, then perform a check of all the hardware and send new
NotifyEvents with AvailabilityState Available when the Charging Station is up and running.
However, there is no required order for a self check and sending a BootNotificationRequest. A
Charging Stations can also do the self check before sending a BootNotificationRequest and
determine the status before a (mobile) network connection is established and a
BootNotificationRequest is sent.
When something is wrong with the Charging Station or EVSE, the status SHALL be set to Faulted.
Reserved and Unavailable states persist after a reboot.

# B01 - Requirements

**B01.FR.01**
Precondition: After start-up.
The Charging Station SHALL send
BootNotificationRequest to the CSMS with
information about its configuration.

**B01.FR.02**
Precondition: B01.FR.01
The CSMS has received
BootNotificationRequest from the
Charging Station.
The CSMS SHALL respond to indicate whether it
will accept the Charging Station.

**B01.FR.03**
Precondition: After a reboot (for instance due to a
remote reset command, power
outage, firmware update, software
error etc.)
The Charging Station SHALL again connect to the
CSMS and SHALL send a BootNotificationRequest
each time it boots or reboots.

**B01.FR.04**
Precondition: When the CSMS responds with
BootNotificationResponse with the
status Accepted AND
interval > 0
The Charging Station SHALL adjust the heartbeat
interval in accordance with the interval from the
response message.

**B01.FR.05**
Precondition: When the CSMS responds with
BootNotificationResponse with the
status Accepted.
The Charging Station SHALL send a
StatusNotificationRequest for each Connector with
its current state.

**B01.FR.06**
Precondition: The Charging Station has received
BootNotificationResponse.
AND
Charging Station is configured to use
Heartbeats for time synchronization
TimeSource
The Charging Station SHALL synchronize the
Charging Station’s internal clock with the supplied
CSMS’s current time.

**B01.FR.07**
Precondition: When a Charging Station or an EVSE is
set to status Unavailable by a
Change Availability command.
The Unavailable status MUST be persistent
across reboots.

**B01.FR.08**
Precondition: Between the physical power-on/reboot
and the successful completion of a
BootNotification, where the CSMS
returns Accepted or Pending.
The Charging Station SHALL NOT send any other
OCPP requests to the CSMS (except
BootNotificationRequest). This includes cached
OCPP messages that are still present in the
Charging Station from prior sessions.

**B01.FR.09**
Precondition: B01.FR.01
The Charging Station SHALL indicate the reason for
sending the BootNotificationRequest message in
the reason field.

# B01 - Requirements (continued)

**B01.FR.10**
Precondition: The Charging Station has received a
BootNotificationResponse in which
status is not Accepted AND
the Charging Station sends a RPC
Framework: CALL message that is
NOT a BootNotificationRequest or a
message triggered by one of the
following messages:
TriggerMessageRequest,
GetBaseReportRequest,
GetReportRequest.
The CSMS SHALL respond with RPC Framework:
CALLERROR: SecurityError.

**B01.FR.11**
Precondition: B01.FR.01 AND
Security profile 3 is used
The CSMS SHALL check the SerialNumber in the
BootNotificationRequest against the Serial Number
in the Certificate Common Name.

**B01.FR.12**
Precondition: B01.FR.11 AND
the SerialNumber in the
BootNotificationRequest does NOT
equal the Serial Number in the
Certificate Common Name
The CSMS SHALL close WebSocket connection.

**B01.FR.13**
Precondition: When an EVSE has been reserved
The Reserved state MUST be persistent across
reboots.
